input_candidates.truth_value: Tensile cracks nucleate at α/γ interfaces and propagate along ND. When encountering a δ ferrite band parallel to RD, the crack is deflected to propagate along RD. The crack may then blunt within the soft δ ferrite due to dislocation emission and local plastic dissipation.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Cracks nucleate at α/γ interfaces, propagate along ND, are captured and deflected by δ ferrite bands parallel to RD, then blunt via dislocation emission and local plastic dissipation.

input_candidates.truth_value: Strain incompatibility arises at ferrite/austenite interfaces due to strength/hardness difference; decreased fraction of ferrite and full recrystallization in DP1000-5 improve compatibility.
input_candidates.prediction_value: In DP950 samples, incompatible straining at ferrite/austenite interfaces due to large strength/hardness difference causes easy crack initiation; in DP1000-5, reduced ferrite fraction and fully recrystallized austenite enable more compatible deformation.

input_candidates.truth_value: During annealing at 950 °C, austenite partially transforms to ferrite, Cr7C3, and intergranular κ-carbides. Above 950 °C, ferrite transforms back to austenite, and carbides dissolve; at 1000 °C, complete recrystallization and dissolution of detrimental carbides, while high Mn/Al/C in austenite leads to fine κ-carbide reprecipitation during water quenching.
input_candidates.prediction_value: With increasing annealing temperature from 950°C to 1050°C, ferrite fraction decreases, Cr7C3 and intergranular κ-carbides dissolve, austenite recrystallizes and grain growth occurs as α ferrite restraint diminishes.

input_candidates.truth_value: The previous record SUTS (169.9 MPa·g^{-1}·cm^{3}) was held by a 29.05Mn-12.01Al-1.48C-5.13Cr austenitic steel with TE 43.8% and density 6.34 g·cm^{-3}.
